title: Stepper
description: Guided flows with numbered or icon indicators, optional descriptions, horizontal or vertical rails, and linear gating so users finish prerequisites before jumping ahead.
intro: Drive the UI with `activeStepIndex` and `(stepChange)`. Set `linear` when steps must be completed in order, and mutate each `Step`‚Äôs `completed`, `error`, `warning`, or `disabled` flags to mirror validation. Use `showDescriptions` for subtitled milestones and `orientation="vertical"` beside a detail pane.

featureSections.title: Navigation without linear gating
featureSections.description: Bind `activeStepIndex` from the parent and let users jump across steps when `linear` is false. Pair with footer buttons that simply move the index.

featureSections.title: Linear completion
featureSections.description: With `linear` true, clicking a future step requires every prior step marked `completed`. Primary actions should set `completed` on the current step before advancing the index.

featureSections.title: Vertical rail
featureSections.description: Stack steps in reading order for side-by-side layouts. Descriptions clarify each milestone while the main column holds the heavy UI.

featureSections.title: Step signals
featureSections.description: `completed`, `error`, `warning`, and `disabled` change indicator color and behavior. Turn off `clickable` when the strip is purely read-only.

featureSections.title: Onboarding-style wizard
featureSections.description: Combine the stepper with fields, consent, inline validation, and a summary: `MessageBar`, `Text`, `Checkbox`, and `Button` mirror how product teams ship multi-step setup without bespoke chrome.

accessibility.title: Step control
accessibility.body: Each step is a `role="button"` with `tabindex="0"` when enabled. The active step sets `aria-current="step"`; disabled steps use `aria-disabled`.

accessibility.title: Linear order
accessibility.body: In linear mode, clicks that would skip incomplete work are ignored‚Äîkeep primary actions in the content area so keyboard and pointer users get the same guardrails.

apiSections.title: StepperComponent - ui-stepper
apiSections.lead: Imperative helpers (`nextStep`, `previousStep`, `goToStep`, `reset`) update internal state and emit `stepChange`; they still respect `linear` and disabled steps.

blocks.title: Inputs
rows: Step definitions including labels and state flags.
Syncs the focused step when within range.
`numeric` checkmarks when complete, or `icon` placeholder markup.
Block forward navigation until prior steps are `completed`.

blocks.title: Outputs
rows: Fired when the active step changes from clicks or imperative API.
Emitted together with `stepChange` after a successful `onStepClick`.

blocks.title: Models
rows: Finished state; drives checkmark and linear rules.
